title: Gov. Sherrill signs bill to begin nuclear procurement in N.J.
summary: On July 13, New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill signed the Power NJ Act, a bill that directs the state’s Board of Public Utilities (BPU), in collaboration with the state’s Economic Development Authority, to establish an “advanced nuclear energy procurement program.”

title: House subcommittee OKs six nuclear permitting reform bills
summary: The U.S. House Energy and Commerce Committee’s Subcommittee on Energy introduced six nuclear permitting reform bills back in June. The mostly bipartisan bills returned to the subcommittee on Tuesday for markups—and with strong backing from both sides of the aisle. Despite some lingering concerns in areas like federal transparency, the role of the Advisory Committee on Reactor Safeguards (ACRS), and staff attrition in the Nuclear Regulatory Commission, the subcommittee advanced all six pieces of legislation to the full committee with bipartisan support.

title: Story named director of industrial accounts at Entergy Arkansas
summary: Entergy Arkansas has promoted Bentley Story to director of industrial accounts.

title: Researchers demonstrate remote real-time adjustment of PUR-1 reactor
summary: The remote, automatic, real-time adjustment of a research reactor’s power by a geographically distributed control system has been reported by researchers from Idaho National Laboratory, the Grainger College of Engineering at the University of Illinois–Urbana-Champaign, and Purdue University. The investigators first used an automatic adjustment “digital control loop” system to demonstrate remote adjustments of Purdue’s PUR-1 research reactor. They then applied a reinforcement learning model that simulates the interaction of physical forces inside the reactor, making the loop system more autonomous.

title: Ebb and flow of wholesale energy prices detailed in ISO-NE charts
summary: Variables like the weather, consumer demand, and fuel costs mean the price of wholesale electricity is in constant flux. The post Ebb and flow of wholesale energy prices detailed in ISO-NE charts appeared first on ISO Newswire.

title: Entergy and MHI Group outline collaborative partnership designed to accelerate commercialization and affordability around carbon capture solutions
summary: Entergy and MHI Group take a leadership position in advancing decarbonization with innovative gas turbine combined cycle and carbon dioxide capture and storage solutions to support sustainable and affordable clean power generation.

title: A reliability update for customers in New Orleans East
summary: Entergy New Orleans continues to make progress on reliability improvements in the Kenilworth area of New Orleans East. This work is focused on strengthening the underground electric system by replacing critical equipment that will help improve reliability and reduce the likelihood of future outages.

title: Summer 2026 update on regional transmission investment now available; 3 projects under construction in New England
summary: One upgrade has been placed in service since March 2026, according to the latest update to the Regional System Plan. The post Summer 2026 update on regional transmission investment now available; 3 projects under construction in New England appeared first on ISO Newswire.

title: Global liquefied natural gas trade volumes reached record high in 2025
summary: EIA says global LNG trade hit a record in 2025 and U.S. LNG exports rose sharply, supporting LNG infrastructure and gas-exposed assets, while Strait of Hormuz disruptions and sanctions still create supply-chain risk.
claims: Global LNG trade reached a record 56.3 Bcf/d in 2025.
U.S. LNG exports rose 26% to 15.1 Bcf/d and EIA says 2026 flows were slowed by Hormuz-related disruption.
